name: paper-hyperconn-vision
description: Use web_search to search for 'hyper-connections computer vision image classification performance results'. Results overlap with earlier searches. How do hyper-connections perform on vision tasks? Summarize results and compare to residual baselines. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
Image Classification (ImageNet, ViT/16): With expansion rate n=2, ViT/16-Base (85M params) improved from 76.38% to 77.60% (SHC) and 77.26% (DHC). ViT/16-Large (307M params) improved from 77.25% to 78.38% (SHC) and 79.94% (DHC), a substantial +2.69% gain. DHC outperformed SHC on the larger model, suggesting dynamic connections benefit more at scale.

Image Generation (DiT-XL/2): SHC x2 improved FID from 2.36 to 2.18, achieving comparable performance to DiT models with 50% more parameters. The gains in vision classification diminished with more training epochs over the same dataset, suggesting hyper-connections provide the most benefit in data-rich regimes.
key_facts: ViT/16-Base accuracy improved from 76.38% to 77.60% (SHC) and 77.26% (DHC) with n=2
ViT/16-Large accuracy improved from 77.25% to 79.94% (DHC), a +2.69% gain
DiT-XL/2 image generation FID improved from 2.36 to 2.18 with SHC x2
DHC outperformed SHC on larger ViT model, with SHC better on smaller model
Vision classification gains diminished with more epochs over the same dataset
